claim_policy.rule: Risk signals are not treated as final target claims without authoritative source evidence, identifier confidence and scope proof.
claim_policy.memo_language: Use source, source_status, checked_at, freshness, fallback_used, confidence, limitation and diligence question for every memo claim.

cyber_infrastructure.title: Cyber infrastructure evidence
cyber_infrastructure.summary: 2 infrastructure item(s) are classified as third-party edge/provider context, not target-owned exposure.
cyber_infrastructure.source: Cloudflare DoH + Shodan InternetDB + IANA/RIR RDAP

rows.claim: 104.18.39.116 resolves through Cloudflare with ports 80, 443, 2052, 2082, 2083 +6 more; no CVE signal returned.
rows.limitation: Provider/CDN ports are not target-owned exposure without additional asset proof.
rows.diligence_question: Confirm CDN/WAF/hosting provider scope and whether any origin servers bypass this edge layer.

rows.claim: 172.64.148.140 resolves through Cloudflare with ports 80, 443, 2082, 2083, 2086 +5 more; no CVE signal returned.
rows.limitation: Provider/CDN ports are not target-owned exposure without additional asset proof.
rows.diligence_question: Confirm CDN/WAF/hosting provider scope and whether any origin servers bypass this edge layer.

diligence_questions: Confirm CDN/WAF/hosting provider scope and whether any origin servers bypass this edge layer.
Ask for a list of origin servers and confirm whether public DNS exposes any bypass around CDN/WAF protection.

sanctions_screening.title: Sanctions and PEP screening evidence
sanctions_screening.summary: No claim-safe sanctions/PEP match was found for Wise plc via sanctions_enricher_v1.

rows.claim: No claim-safe sanctions or PEP risk match was found for Wise plc.
rows.limitation: Screening is point-in-time and depends on available identifiers, transliteration and dataset freshness.
rows.diligence_question: Confirm legal name, registration number, UBOs and directors before final compliance sign-off.

diligence_questions: Confirm legal name, registration number, UBOs and directors before final compliance sign-off.
Are UBOs, directors and material related parties also in scope for sanctions/PEP screening?

finance_forensics.title: Finance forensic evidence
finance_forensics.summary: Finance forensic evidence for Wise plc verifies the Benford analyzer route, but no target-specific Benford conclusion is available yet.
finance_forensics.source: DueSight Financial Bridge + Benford Analyzer + DE Register Filing Gate

rows.claim: Benford analyzer route completed for Wise plc on a deterministic smoke population. Only 0 target-specific finance values were extractable, below the 30-value minimum. Route-smoke metrics: chi2=0.0282, MAD=0.0142, n=64, deviation=LOW.
rows.limitation: This verifies that the Benford analyzer path works; it is not a target-specific finance finding and cannot support a memo conclusion.
rows.diligence_question: Collect an official filing, XBRL extract, P&L export or transaction-level population before using Benford as target evidence.

diligence_questions: Collect an official filing, XBRL extract, P&L export or transaction-level population before using Benford as target evidence.

ownership_register.title: Ownership and register evidence
ownership_register.summary: No memo-safe ownership/register conclusion is available for Wise plc.

rows.claim: GLEIF returned LEI register context for Wise plc: WISE PLC (LEI 213800LD9XCHIC1C4V71).
rows.limitation: GLEIF is strong legal-entity context, but it does not replace official KvK Basisprofiel identity verification for Dutch entities.
rows.diligence_question: Does the LEI record match the exact legal entity, jurisdiction and registration number used in the deal documents?

rows.claim: Ownership proxy built partial ownership-tree context for Wise plc.
rows.source: DueSight Algorithmic UBO Proxy / OpenKvK, GLEIF Level 2
rows.limitation: Partial tree context is not a complete UBO conclusion and must be reconciled against official/control documents.
rows.diligence_question: Which ownership links are unsupported, stale or missing percentages in the reconstructed tree?

diligence_questions: Does the LEI record match the exact legal entity, jurisdiction and registration number used in the deal documents?
Which ownership links are unsupported, stale or missing percentages in the reconstructed tree?

regulatory_exposure.title: Regulatory exposure evidence
regulatory_exposure.summary: No regulatory-register hit was found for Wise plc across the checked routes, with scope limitations.

rows.claim: No regulatory-register hit was found for Wise plc in the checked routes.
rows.limitation: No-hit is not proof that no regulatory exposure exists; source availability, country scope and name matching limit coverage.
rows.diligence_question: Should the target be regulated based on activity, geography, license perimeter or group structure?

diligence_questions: Should the target be regulated based on activity, geography, license perimeter or group structure?

reputation_adverse_media.title: Reputation and adverse media evidence
reputation_adverse_media.summary: Reputation evidence for Wise plc includes target-relevant adverse-media signals requiring source review.

rows.claim: Adverse-media route found 10 target-relevant article signal(s) for Wise plc. Categories: fraude, regulatory, legal. Example title: Wise fined in US by state regulators for anti-money laundering compliance failures - MLex.
rows.source: DueSight adverse-media source ladder (public news + legal metadata)
rows.limitation: Adverse media is a triage signal unless confirmed against authoritative sources, exact entity identifiers and publication context.
rows.diligence_question: Which article, regulator/court source, publication date and entity identifiers make the adverse-media signal deal-relevant?

diligence_questions: Which article, regulator/court source, publication date and entity identifiers make the adverse-media signal deal-relevant?

rows.limitation: Inventory row only; exact entity/person screening still requires deterministic matching output.

rows.claim: Local registry DuckDB is present with 2 cached registry rows.
rows.limitation: Small cache; not enough for final UBO/shareholder closure without target-specific registry extract or graph provider output.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: Adverse-media cache is present with 19 files and 1 target cache hits.
rows.limitation: Cached no-hit supports guarded media context only; complete clearance requires source list, query scope and timestamp.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: Whale tracker cache is present with 5 snapshots.
rows.limitation: Portfolio/institutional-flow context only; usually not material for private NL targets without a matched security/issuer.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: NVD/CVE cache is present with 13 files.
rows.limitation: Cache availability is not target exposure proof; combine with tech fingerprinting, asset ownership and CVE matching.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: DueSight master DuckDB is present with 120 cert/cache rows.
rows.limitation: Internal cache inventory only; does not replace target-specific primary evidence.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: Award intelligence module is not present at the expected first-class module path.
rows.limitation: FT1000/revenue/distress building blocks exist, but the lost award_intel module must be rebuilt before award claims enter standard reports.
rows.diligence_question: Rebuild award_db_builder, shadow_pl_award_bridge, award_intel_tool and seed_data before using award/gazelle claims.

diligence_questions: Which target-specific module output turns local source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: Local Yente/OpenSanctions backend is OK with 349 catalog datasets and searchable=True.
rows.limitation: Inventory row only; exact sanctions/PEP conclusions require a target/person match row.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: Local OFAC/sanctions DuckDB is present with 19050 sanctions records.
rows.limitation: Inventory row only; exact entity/person screening still requires deterministic matching output.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: Local registry DuckDB is present with 2 cached registry rows.
rows.limitation: Small cache; not enough for final UBO/shareholder closure without target-specific registry extract or graph provider output.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: Adverse-media cache is present with 19 files and 1 target cache hits.
rows.limitation: Cached no-hit supports guarded media context only; complete clearance requires source list, query scope and timestamp.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: Whale tracker cache is present with 5 snapshots.
rows.limitation: Portfolio/institutional-flow context only; usually not material for private NL targets without a matched security/issuer.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: NVD/CVE cache is present with 13 files.
rows.limitation: Cache availability is not target exposure proof; combine with tech fingerprinting, asset ownership and CVE matching.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: DueSight master DuckDB is present with 120 cert/cache rows.
rows.limitation: Internal cache inventory only; does not replace target-specific primary evidence.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: Award intelligence module is not present at the expected first-class module path.
rows.limitation: FT1000/revenue/distress building blocks exist, but the lost award_intel module must be rebuilt before award claims enter standard reports.
rows.diligence_question: Rebuild award_db_builder, shadow_pl_award_bridge, award_intel_tool and seed_data before using award/gazelle claims.

rows.claim: No regulatory-register hit was found for Wise plc in the checked routes.
rows.limitation: No-hit is not proof that no regulatory exposure exists; source availability, country scope and name matching limit coverage.
rows.diligence_question: Should the target be regulated based on activity, geography, license perimeter or group structure?

rows.claim: Adverse-media route found 10 target-relevant article signal(s) for Wise plc. Categories: fraude, regulatory, legal. Example title: Wise fined in US by state regulators for anti-money laundering compliance failures - MLex.
rows.source: DueSight adverse-media source ladder (public news + legal metadata)
rows.limitation: Adverse media is a triage signal unless confirmed against authoritative sources, exact entity identifiers and publication context.
rows.diligence_question: Which article, regulator/court source, publication date and entity identifiers make the adverse-media signal deal-relevant?

diligence_questions: Confirm CDN/WAF/hosting provider scope and whether any origin servers bypass this edge layer.
Ask for a list of origin servers and confirm whether public DNS exposes any bypass around CDN/WAF protection.
Confirm legal name, registration number, UBOs and directors before final compliance sign-off.
Are UBOs, directors and material related parties also in scope for sanctions/PEP screening?
Collect an official filing, XBRL extract, P&L export or transaction-level population before using Benford as target evidence.
Does the LEI record match the exact legal entity, jurisdiction and registration number used in the deal documents?
Which ownership links are unsupported, stale or missing percentages in the reconstructed tree?
Should the target be regulated based on activity, geography, license perimeter or group structure?
Which article, regulator/court source, publication date and entity identifiers make the adverse-media signal deal-relevant?
Which profile claims are official-source confirmed versus inferred from search/news?
